draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a strictly professional, minimalist interface over conversational tone
facade.label: All business, no chat
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should use formal language, clean typography, zero personality or emoji, focus on efficiency and credibility
facade.rejectImplies: Users want some warmth, conversational elements, or personality injected into the interface—friendliness matters alongside professionalism

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer a strictly professional, minimalist interface over conversational tone
evidence.implication: The workspace should use formal language, clean typography, zero personality or emoji, focus on efficiency and credibility

facade.hypothesis: Users might prefer warm, diffused lighting that feels calming over harsh contrast
facade.label: Soft shadows and glow
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward ambient, layered lighting; muted color temperature; gentle depth cues; reduces visual fatigue
facade.rejectImplies: Preference for clarity over mood; possibly more minimalist; may want higher contrast or cooler tones instead

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer low-contrast, reduced visual noise over bright or colorful designs
facade.label: Minimal dark interface
facade.acceptImplies: The builder values focus, reduced eye strain, and a tech-forward aesthetic; likely prefers dark mode, sparse layouts, and high contrast text
facade.rejectImplies: The builder may prefer warmer tones, more visual texture, or higher information density; possibly concerned that minimal dark feels cold or sterile for collaborative workspace

facade.hypothesis: Users expect immediate visual feedback on every action, with zero perceptible delay between intent and result
facade.axisTargeted: Gesture responsiveness — snappy click-feedback vs. fluid animated transition
facade.label: Instant response
facade.acceptImplies: Build with eager, sub-100ms feedback loops. Click = immediate highlight/state change. No loading states. Cursor follows finger instantly.
facade.rejectImplies: Embrace slight delay and transition. Actions animate over 200-400ms. Loading states are visible. Feedback is smooth, not snappy.

facade.hypothesis: Users working in AI tools prefer dense metric displays over narrative summaries
facade.label: Dashboard with numbers
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should lead with quantified data, KPIs, and real-time stats. Users want to scan metrics fast. Charts and grids over prose.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want context and story around data. Narrative, explanations, and key insights matter more than raw numbers. Prose, cards, and guided reading over dense dashboards.

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces need to choose between persistent left navigation (sidebar) vs top-level section switching (tabs) — this is the foundational layout tension.
facade.label: Sidebar or tabs
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward persistent, always-visible navigation — likely wants quick jumping between AI tools, chat history, settings without losing context.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers minimalist top chrome — suggests they want focus on content area, less visual hierarchy, maybe prefer modal/overlay access to secondary controls.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users actually prefer a friendly, approachable tone in their AI workspace to build trust and reduce friction
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality: professional vs friendly
facade.label: Conversational and warm
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should use casual language, personality, maybe occasional emoji, warm color palettes, and a tone that feels like working with a helpful colleague rather than a corporate tool
facade.rejectImplies: Confirms that professional minimalism is the right direction; users want their AI workspace to feel serious, credible, and out of the way rather than personable

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
